channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck
item.description: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

media:group.media:description: Alex Márquez suffered a fractured C7 vertebrae and a broken collarbone.

item.title: Federal government steps in on China-linked Northern Minerals investors
item.description: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Northern Minerals operates the Browns Range mine in WA.

item.title: Woman jailed for drunkenly setting friend on fire appeals sentence
item.description: Corbie Walpole's legal team has argued the sentencing judge failed to properly consider psychiatric evidence about her mental health at the time of the offending.
item.dc:creator: Ashlee Aldridge

media:group.media:description: Corbie Jean Walpole (centre) has appealed her seven year jail sentence in the Court of Criminal Appeal.

item.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'
item.description: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

media:group.media:description: The site's aging blast furnace has been out of action since last month.

item.title: 'Frightening' near-miss between truck, motorcycles under investigation
item.description: Police are investigating after dashcam footage emerged of a large truck narrowly avoiding hitting two motorcyclists on a rural road in Tasmania.

media:group.media:description: Truck almost hits motorcyclists

item.title: Stronger bait for farmers approved amid mouse plague
item.description: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Prendergast and Chris Lewis

media:group.media:description: Mice are running rampant across parts of WA's wheatbelt.

item.title: Inside the deadly search to recover four divers' bodies from a deep-sea Maldives cave
item.description: Expert divers have been sent to the Maldives to retrieve four bodies in a deep-sea cave network, in a search mission that's already killed one military rescue diver.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Bowles

media:group.media:description: Monica Montefalcone is one of five Italian divers who died in the Maldives while exploring an underwater cave.

item.title: Plans for largest wind farm in Southern Hemisphere get Victorian approval
item.description: An environmental advocacy group and a council mayor say the Victorian government has not properly considered the potential impacts of building a major new wind farm in Western Victoria.
item.dc:creator: Nethma Dandeniya

media:group.media:description: The new wind farm will be located near the already existing Murra Warra Wind Farm.

item.title: Plan to build Sydney's newest Chinatown in 'underutilised' area revealed
item.description: The Parramatta City Council is planning a new Chinatown in an "under-utilised" part of the Western Sydney CBD, but community members want to see more than just food businesses.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the plan shows the southern side of Church Street adorned with Chinese lanterns.

item.title: Hundreds of holidaymakers in limbo as travel company cancels trips
item.description: Australian travellers say they have been dumped days and weeks before their overseas holidays after a tour company cancelled their trips.
item.dc:creator: Kristy Sexton-McGrath

media:group.media:description: Elizabeth and John Jennings say they spent weeks unsuccessfully trying to obtain tickets for their 11-day China tour.

item.title: Hindley climbs into podium contention, Giro 'not over' despite Vingegaard win
item.description: Jai Hindley insists that the Giro d'Italia is not over despite Jonas Vinegagaard proving his mountain dominance with another impressive victory on stage nine.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jai Hindley (right) crossed the line ahead of Michael Storer.

item.title: 'Bro, I've got a frog in my lettuce': Woolworths investigates salad bag find
item.description: A Kiwi couple in a remote port town on WA's south coast have shared an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.
item.dc:creator: Mark Gibson and Andrew Williams

media:group.media:description: A live green frog inside a lettuce bag bought from Woolworths in Esperance, WA.

item.title: Person dies as fire in north Canberra unit causes 'substantial damage'
item.description: Emergency services were called to the Wallcliffe Flats in Belconnen at about 6:30am where they found a ground-floor unit alight.

media:group.media:description: The ground-floor unit suffered "substantial damage" in the blaze.

item.title: What to know about the current Ebola outbreak in Uganda and the Congo
item.description: The WHO has declared a global health emergency after more than 80 cases of Ebola were confirmed across two African countries. Here's why the organisation is calling for preparedness, not panic.
item.dc:creator: Georgie Hewson

media:group.media:description: A man arrives at Bunia General Referral Hospital following confirmation of an Ebola outbreak involving the Bundibugyo strain in Bunia.

item.title: Murder accused to remain in custody after man's death north of Bundaberg
item.description: Yutthawichan Phetrchaisak is remanded in custody after being charged with the murder of a 42-year-old man north of Bundaberg.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: A 33-year-old man was charged with murder on Sunday.

item.title: Miles declares 'a win is a win' as Labor squeaks by in by-election
item.description: Labor candidate Luke Richmond will become the next member for Stafford, but the party's vote dropped several points.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Steven Miles has led Labor to a win in the Stafford by-election, but suffered a 4.1 per cent swing away from the party to the LNP.

item.title: Church warns 'everything's on the table' as redress bill continues to climb
item.description: Tasmania's Anglican Church says what it expects to pay in compensation to victim-survivors of child sexual abuse has more than doubled in the past three years.
item.dc:creator: Loretta Lohberger

media:group.media:description: Richard Condie says the church's redress working group will meet to consider how to raise further fund.

item.title: Young woman killed, three injured in northern NSW crash
item.description: Police are investigating after a car flipped after crashing into a power pole.
item.dc:creator: Marina Trajkovich

media:group.media:description: NSW Police tap generic shot as thumbnail image.

item.title: Taiwan president responds after Trump's visit to China
item.description: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

media:group.media:description: Lai Ching-te says Taiwan will not give up its democratic and free way of life.

item.title: 'Make it legal next time': Another blistering 100m for Aussie Nketia
item.description: For the second time this year, Eddie Nketia stops the clock in what would be an Australian record for the 100m, but is aided by an illegal tailwind.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Edward Nketia won the 100m in 9.74 seconds with an illegal tailwind of 5.6 metres per second.

item.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling
item.description: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.
item.dc:creator: Jane Rocca

media:group.media:description: Rochelle Gregory, who runs fashion label Rocky Rafaela, has laid bare her mental health battle in a short film screened during her latest runway show.

item.title: Man found dead after fire at block of units in Ballarat's Wendouree
item.description: The man was pulled from the house by emergency crews, but could not be revived.
item.dc:creator: Laura Mayers and Coco Veldkamp

media:group.media:description: Police are investigating a fatal house fire in the Ballarat suburb of Wendouree this morning.

item.title: Bid to reopen Albany hydrotherapy pool rejected
item.description: The WA health minister rejects recommissioning Albany's public hydrotherapy pool despite a feasibility study saying there is a justifiable cause to do so.
item.dc:creator: Atlas Cook

media:group.media:description: Glenys Nottle is one of 100 people who would make use of the hydrotherapy pool.

item.title: Pilots parachute from jets after mid-flight collision at US air show
item.description: Two fighter jets have collided mid-air during an air show at a US Air Force base, forcing pilots to eject and parachute to safety.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: The two Growlers collided in mid-air, prompting the crew to bail out over Mountain Home Air Force Base.

item.title: 1.2 million Queensland homes without compliant smoke alarms near deadline
item.description: A national company estimates many Queensland homes are unprepared as the deadline to install compliant smoke alarms nears.
item.dc:creator: Sally Rafferty

media:group.media:description: Brandon Lewis lost everything in a house fire in 2014.
